Title: Divergence of a genomic island leads to the evolution of melanization in a halophyte root fungus

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: group 1 and group 2 isolates; JP-R-44 reference genome
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with saline adaptation in fungus
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: melanocyte-stimulating hormone receptor; integral membrane receptor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to contribute to increased melanin biosynthesis in L. rhizohalophila and showed strong selective sweep signal
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population genomics (Tajima's D, XP-CLR) and cluster context analysis; phenotypic association with melanin accumulation under salinity
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: pks2; mfs; transcription factor ID 2358; transcription factor ID 2363; expansin protein ID 2360
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation: BioProject PRJNA517533, BioSample SAMN10836428, GenBank SELF00000000; resequencing SRA SRR8569110-SRR8569138
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mshr lies in the positively selected melanin-associated genomic island. 2. It showed a strong selective sweep signature in group 1 (XP-CLR 39.7; Tajima's D -1.83). 3. The authors speculate that MSHR contributes to increased melanin biosynthesis, although direct mechanistic validation was not provided.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes MSHR is known from mammals and speculates on a conserved melanization principle in fungi; function in this fungus remains inferential.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: group 1 and group 2 isolates; JP-R-44 reference genome
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fungal adaptation to salinity stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to mediate melanin transport/trafficking contributing to melanization differences between populations
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population genomics localization in divergent genomic island; LD analysis around mfs; phenotypic association with melanin accumulation under salinity
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: pks2; mshr; transcription factor ID 2358; transcription factor ID 2363; expansin protein ID 2360
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation: BioProject PRJNA517533, BioSample SAMN10836428, GenBank SELF00000000; resequencing SRA SRR8569110-SRR8569138
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mfs is part of the six-gene melanin-associated genomic island. 2. The authors propose that MFS transports melanin from the cytosol to the cell wall. 3. Strong LD surrounding mfs in group 1 supports its inclusion in the selective sweep region.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport role is inferred from cluster context and known secondary metabolite gene cluster organization; no direct transport assay was reported.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: group 1 and group 2 isolates; JP-R-44 reference genome
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fungal adaptation to salinity stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to facilitate melanin deposition/localization by loosening the fungal cell wall
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population genomics localization in divergent genomic island; inference from physical clustering and known expansin function
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: pks2; mshr; mfs; transcription factor ID 2358; transcription factor ID 2363
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation: BioProject PRJNA517533, BioSample SAMN10836428, GenBank SELF00000000; resequencing SRA SRR8569110-SRR8569138
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The expansin-like gene is part of the melanin-associated genomic island. 2. The authors hypothesize that it loosens the fungal cell wall for increased melanin deposition. 3. Direct functional evidence for this role was not provided.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role remains unclear and speculative in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: group 1 and group 2 isolates; JP-R-44 reference genome
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fungal adaptation to salinity stress; linked to improved growth under saline conditions
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type I fungal polyketide synthase; predicted KS, AT, ACP, and TD domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first step of melanin biosynthesis in the proposed DHN-melanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms early pathway intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased melanin accumulation in group 1 under 2% NaCl and with saline adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population genomics (Fst, Tajima's D, XP-CLR, LD); in silico antiSMASH domain prediction; melanin inhibitor assay supporting DHN-melanin pathway; metabolite/phenotype comparison of melanin accumulation under salinity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2% NaCl salinity stress; melanin biosynthesis inhibitors tricyclazole and kojic acid
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: mshr; mfs; transcription factor ID 2358; transcription factor ID 2363; expansin protein ID 2360
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation: BioProject PRJNA517533, BioSample SAMN10836428, GenBank SELF00000000; resequencing SRA SRR8569110-SRR8569138
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pks2 is located in a highly divergent ~20 kbp genomic island within a putative melanin biosynthetic cluster. 2. pks2 showed strong signatures of positive selection in group 1 (XP-CLR 85.4; Tajima's D -1.85) and strong local LD. 3. The paper proposes PKS2 catalyzes the first step of DHN-melanin biosynthesis and contributes to higher melanin accumulation and saline adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Group 1 carried 10 fixed or nearly fixed nonsynonymous SNPs in pks2. Distinguished from pks1, which appears impaired.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for studying fungal saline adaptation and melanization mechanisms; engineering value not directly discussed in the paper

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: group 1 and group 2 isolates; JP-R-44 reference genome
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fungal adaptation to salinity stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Proposed regulator within melanin biosynthetic gene cluster
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the six-gene genomic island proposed to coordinate melanization-associated phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with melanin biosynthesis differences between populations under salinity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population genomics localization within positively selected divergent genomic island; phenotypic association with melanin accumulation under 2% NaCl
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: pks2; mshr; mfs; transcription factor ID 2363; expansin protein ID 2360
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation: BioProject PRJNA517533, BioSample SAMN10836428, GenBank SELF00000000; resequencing SRA SRR8569110-SRR8569138
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TF ID 2358 is one of two transcription factors located in the divergent melanin-associated genomic island. 2. The paper proposes that it contributes to regulation of the melanin biosynthetic cluster. 3. Direct target genes and mechanism were not experimentally resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a cluster component likely involved in regulation; no direct functional assay for this TF was reported.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: group 1 and group 2 isolates; JP-R-44 reference genome
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fungal adaptation to salinity stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Proposed regulator within melanin biosynthetic gene cluster
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the six-gene genomic island proposed to coordinate melanization-associated phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with melanin biosynthesis differences between populations under salinity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population genomics localization within positively selected divergent genomic island; phenotypic association with melanin accumulation under 2% NaCl
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: pks2; mshr; mfs; transcription factor ID 2358; expansin protein ID 2360
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation: BioProject PRJNA517533, BioSample SAMN10836428, GenBank SELF00000000; resequencing SRA SRR8569110-SRR8569138
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TF ID 2363 is the second transcription factor in the divergent melanin-associated genomic island. 2. It is proposed to regulate genes required for melanization in the cluster. 3. Its direct regulatory targets were not tested experimentally.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a likely regulatory component only; no direct functional assay was provided.
